title: rubus × amphitapus
intro: Rubus × amphitapus, a hybrid of the bramble and raspberry plants, is a type of thorny shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus × amphitapus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
